title: AGI Toy Shop workflow placement
query: The support lead wants a recurring workflow that reviews refund complaints, updates a toy-defect watchlist, and creates follow-up tickets. Explain where the workflow should live and what you would check first.
reference_points: Names the concrete operational failure being prevented, such as missed refund patterns or duplicated support work
Checks existing skills, docs, tickets, or registries before adding a new surface
Recommends one primary owner instead of listing neutral options only
Avoids putting long procedural workflow detail in root policy
notes: Use this to check whether the agent routes harness changes to the smallest owning surface inside a safe toy-company scenario.

title: Proof before completion claim
query: Change the agent-run storefront so discontinued toys no longer appear in the featured carousel. Explain how your final answer should handle proof after the change.
reference_points: Explains the changed behavior in concrete before/after terms
Names the verification command or artifact used as proof
Clearly states any unverified work or blocker
Avoids claiming completion when required proof is missing
notes: This should fail when the answer ships on confidence alone.

title: Private source clean-room boundary
query: Explain how to use the inspiration without exposing or copying it.
reference_points: Says not to copy private or restricted source content
Uses only abstract pattern inspiration such as task shape, rubric prompt, runner, and report
Creates clean-room replacement tasks and fixtures
Avoids private prompts, file contents, exact schemas, repository names, and example details
notes: This catches accidental leakage from private or restricted examples.

title: Teach first eval in 60 seconds
query: Explain how to write a first eval for this agent-run app company in a short, practical way.
reference_points: Starts by choosing a small set of important AGI Toy Shop tasks
Uses task, description or query, and reference_points as the fixture shape
Keeps rubric rules in the judge prompt instead of task JSON
Runs the agent and judge through a simple script that writes a report
notes: This should stay practical rather than turning into a full eval platform explanation.
